route.name: Boulder Weekend Loop
route.description: Public GPX Front Range weekend loop with large climbs, mixed dirt, pavement, and mountain-town access.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX Front Range weekend loop with large climbs, mixed dirt, pavement, and mountain-town access.
route.bestSeason: July to September is the prime window; verify storms, camping, and Front Range closures.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX Front Range weekend loop with large climbs, mixed dirt, pavement, and mountain-town access.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Nederland has services; streams may be filter options but need final measurement.
resources.food: Boulder and Nederland services; confirm route-day hours.
resources.camping: Free camping near Nederland/Switzerland Trail may work, while paid campgrounds can require reservations.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before ride-ready promotion.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Promote only after exact camp plan is locked.
Use early starts for heat and storms.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control ride-ready promotion.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: reject/rework for ride_ready; exact camps/lodging, ARP order overlay, water-gap table, and Stage 1 fire/storm refresh are unresolved
blockers: Not ride-ready until date-specific camp/permit, water, legal access, closures, fire/smoke, and weather are refreshed.
No exact legal overnight anchor for any July 10-13 night.
Boulder Ranger District Stage 1 restrictions require stove/fire compliance.
ARP orders including Caribou Road, raptor closures, and designated dispersed-camping restrictions require route overlay.
Weather, Red Flag, smoke, and storm refresh is too early on July 2.
requiredFixes: Complete July 9-10 final refresh for next-weekend ride readiness.
Select exact camps/lodging and verify availability for July 10 and/or July 11.
Overlay GPX against ARP Boulder Ranger District closures and designated dispersed camping orders.
Confirm Nederland services and any campground water.
Create a measured water-gap table from the GPX.
Refresh NWS hazards/point forecasts, ARP alerts, AirNow/smoke, and fire restrictions July 8-10 and ride mornings.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?
